name: 3 Day Bromo & Ijen Volcano Discovery from Bali
description: 3-day private Bali-to-Bali loop tour via Java: Gilimanuk–Ketapang ferry to Java (Day 1), Mount Bromo sunrise with private 4WD jeep and Madakaripura Waterfall followed by Ijen health screening (Day 2), Kawah Ijen Blue Fire midnight hike then return ferry Ketapang–Gilimanuk back to Bali (Day 3). Starts and ends in Bali. Health screening coordinated for Ijen crater access under BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024. 100% private. Licensed operator NIB 1102230032918.

item.name: Day 1 — Bali to the Bromo Side via Java Crossing
item.description: Private transfer from Bali. Gilimanuk–Ketapang ferry crossing to Java. Continue to Bromo staging area. Hotel: Joglo Kecombrang Bromo.

item.name: Day 2 — Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Transfer to the Ijen Side
item.description: Bromo stargazing and sunrise over the Tengger caldera (private 4WD jeep). Madakaripura Waterfall canyon descent. Transfer east to Bondowoso. Health screening on arrival at hotel (SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate — BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024). Hotel: Riverside Homestay.

item.name: Day 3 — Ijen Blue Fire Crater and Return to Bali
item.description: Midnight departure to Paltuding base. Guided ascent to Kawah Ijen crater rim (2,386 m). Blue Fire observation in the pre-dawn window. Gas masks and trekking poles provided. Descent, breakfast, then private transfer to Ketapang port. Ferry crossing Ketapang (Java) → Gilimanuk (Bali). Drop-off at agreed Bali location. Tour starts and ends in Bali.

subjectOf.name: Mount Bromo
subjectOf.description: Active volcano in the Tengger Semeru National Park. Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint and crater base accessed by private 4WD jeep. Elevation 2,329 m.

subjectOf.name: Madakaripura Waterfall
subjectOf.description: Indonesia's tallest waterfall (estimated 200 m). Canyon wade through narrow gorge passages. Located in Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park.

subjectOf.name: Kawah Ijen
subjectOf.description: Active stratovolcano with the world's largest acidic crater lake and the world's largest visible blue fire phenomenon. Elevation 2,386 m.

offers.description: Per-person: IDR 4,050,000 (2 pax) · IDR 3,650,000 (3) · IDR 3,400,000 (4–5) · IDR 3,200,000 (6–7) · IDR 3,000,000 (8–10) · IDR 2,850,000 (11+). Minimum 2 guests. All-inclusive: private transport Bali to Bali (round-trip Java), crew, 2 nights accommodation + breakfast, entrance fees, 4WD jeep (Bromo), gas masks + trekking poles (Ijen), health screening, Gilimanuk–Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry crossings, mineral water, T-shirt.

mainEntity.name: What is included in the 3 Day Bromo & Ijen Volcano Discovery from Bali price?

acceptedAnswer.text: All JVTO packages include: private transport (AC MPV for 1–3 guests, Toyota Hiace for 4–9), dedicated crew, all entrance fees and permits, mineral water daily, pick-up to drop-off assistance, and a JVTO travel T-shirt. This Bali-loop tour additionally includes both Gilimanuk–Ketapang and Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry crossings (round-trip Java), 2 nights accommodation with breakfast, a private 4WD jeep for Mount Bromo, gas masks and trekking poles for Kawah Ijen, and health-screening coordination (BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024). International flights, travel insurance, and personal expenses are not included.

mainEntity.name: When does the Ijen health screening happen on this tour?

acceptedAnswer.text: Health screening is coordinated on the evening of Day 2 — after Madakaripura Waterfall and on arrival at the Bondowoso hotel, before the midnight Ijen departure on Day 3. As required under BBKSDA access regulations (Surat Edaran SE.1658/KSA.9/2024), licensed medical staff measure SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate, and respiratory history. The resulting QR-coded certificate is checked by BBKSDA at the crater access gate. No valid QR code means no access to the crater zone.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I do not pass the Ijen health screening?

acceptedAnswer.text: If screening staff or the JVTO team advise that you should not attempt the hike, JVTO follows that decision — it protects your safety and others on the trail. The climb is cancelled for that individual (not the group); alternatives include waiting at base camp or a safe zone. The screening session cost is non-refundable. The group continues.

mainEntity.name: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my tour dates?

acceptedAnswer.text: JVTO provides a written Plan B briefed before departure, not improvised at the gate. If a site closes due to volcanic activity or park authority order, an alternative route is offered. Guests may rebook at no penalty if the primary sites are inaccessible on the booked dates due to official closure. JVTO monitors MAGMA Indonesia / PVMBG and BBKSDA advisories daily and contacts guests proactively if closures are imminent.

mainEntity.name: Does this tour start and end in Bali?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. This is a Bali-to-Bali loop. JVTO picks you up from your agreed Bali location on Day 1, crosses to Java via the Gilimanuk–Ketapang ferry, completes the Java itinerary (Bromo, Madakaripura, Ijen), and returns you to Bali via the Ketapang–Gilimanuk ferry at the end of Day 3. You do not need to arrange any additional transport between Bali and Java — both ferry crossings are included. If you prefer a one-way Surabaya-to-Bali overland option covering Bromo, Madakaripura, and Ijen, see the 3 Day Bromo, Madakaripura & Ijen Overland from Surabaya to Bali.
